However, given its role in assessing metabolic status following suspected hypoxic-ischemic injury during delivery, which could be an indicator of underlying neurological damage rather than just confirming asphyxia itself, estimation remains somewhat relevant albeit secondary compared to other tests such as APGAR scores and cranial ultrasounds.
